sarga.title: Sumantra gives further details of the  story narrated by Sanatkumara--on the advice of Sumantra, king Dasaratha goes to Romapada  requests Rsyasringa's help to conduct aswamedha yaga Rsyasringa comes to Ayodhya.

shlokas.original:
भूय एव हि राजेन्द्र शृणु मे वचनं हितम्।
यथा स देवप्रवर: कथायामेवमब्रवीत्।।1.11.1।।
shlokas.translation: "Great king, I shall tell you the way Sanatkumara, great among the celestials further narrated the story. Listen to my words which may prove useful to you (said Sumantra)".

shlokas.original:
इक्ष्वाकूणां कुले जातो भविष्यति सुधार्मिक:।
राजा दशरथो नाम्ना श्रीमान्सत्यप्रतिश्रव:।।1.11.2।।

अङ्गराजेन सख्यं च तस्य राज्ञो भविष्यति।
कन्या चास्य महाभागा शान्ता नाम भविष्यति।।1.11.3।।
shlokas.translation: That king (Dasaratha) will develop friendship with the king of Anga. A highly virtuous daughter named Santa will be born to  king of Anga.

shlokas.original:
पुत्रस्तु सोऽङ्गराजस्य रोमपाद इति श्रुत:।
तं स राजा दशरथो गमिष्यति महायशा:।।1.11.4।।
shlokas.translation: The famous king Dasaratha will approach the son of the king of Anga known as Romapada.

shlokas.original:
अनपत्योऽस्मि धर्मात्मन् शान्ताभर्ता मम क्रतुम्।
आहरेत त्वयाज्ञप्तस्सन्तानार्थं कुलस्य च।।1.11.5।।
shlokas.translation: O righteous king I am issueless. Let Rsyasringa, husband of Santa be ordered to perform the a sacrificial ceremony so that I shall be blessed with I son for the perpetuaion of my race.

shlokas.original:
श्रुत्वा राज्ञोऽथ तद्वाक्यं मनसा स विचिन्त्य च।
प्रदास्यते पुत्रवन्तं शान्ताभर्तारमात्मवान्।।1.11.6।।
shlokas.translation: On hearing his words and deeply thinking over the matter, the prudent Romapada  agreed to send, Santa's husband (Rsyasringa), capable of blessing him with a son.

shlokas.original:
प्रतिगृह्य च तं विप्रं स राजा विगतज्वर:।
आहरिष्यति तं यज्ञं प्रहृष्टेनान्तरात्मना।।1.11.7।।
shlokas.translation: The king relieved of his suffering, was highly delighted. He would take the brahmin with him and  perform the sacrificial ritual.

shlokas.original:
तं च राजा दशरथो यष्टुकाम: कृताञ्जलि:।
ऋश्यशृङ्गं द्विजश्रेष्ठं वरयिष्यति धर्मवित्।। 1.11.8।। 
यज्ञार्थं प्रसवार्थं च स्वर्गार्थं च नरेश्वर:।
लभते च स तं कामं द्विजमुख्याद्विशांपति:।।1.11.9।।
shlokas.translation: King Dasaratha, lord of the people, and knower of dharma and truth, desirous of performing the sacrifice would ask Rsyasringa, with folded palms for granting a boon to perform a sacrifice in order to obtain children and to attain heaven. The king will get those desires fulfilled through that chief brahmin.

shlokas.original:
पुत्राश्चास्य भविष्यन्ति चत्वारोऽमितविक्रमा:।
वंशप्रतिष्ठानकरास्सर्वलोकेषु विश्रुता:।।1.11.10।।
shlokas.translation: Four sons of immense prowess would be born to him bringing glory and fame to their dynasty. They would be renowned in all the worlds.

shlokas.original:
एवं स देवप्रवर: पूर्वं कथितवान्कथाम्।
सनत्कुमारो भगवान्पुरा देवयुगे प्रभु:।।1.11.11।।
shlokas.translation: In early times of Kritayuga, this story was narrated by the holy and competent  Sanatkumara, the great one among the devatas".

shlokas.original:
स त्वं पुरुषशार्दूल तमानय सुसत्कृतम्।
स्वयमेव महाराज गत्वा सबलवाहन:।।1.11.12।।
shlokas.translation: "Greatest among men, O king, along with vehicles and retinue, you may go and bring him (Rsyasringa) personally with due respect".

shlokas.original:
अनुमान्य वसिष्ठं च सूतवाक्यं निशम्य च।
सान्त:पुरस्सहामात्य: प्रययौ यत्र स द्विज:।।1.11.13।।
shlokas.translation: Having heard the charioteer's words and having obtained the consent of Vasishta, he    along with his queens and courtiers departed to the place where the brahmin (Rsyasringa) resided.

shlokas.original:
वनानि सरितश्चैव व्यतिक्रम्य शनैश्शनै:।
अभिचक्राम तं देशं यत्र वै मुनिपुङ्गव:।।1.11.14।।
shlokas.translation: Crossing forests and rivers slowly, the king reached the place where that foremost of  ascetics lived.

shlokas.original:
आसाद्य तं द्विजश्रेष्ठं रोमपादसमीपगम्।
ऋषिपुत्रं ददर्शादौ दीप्यमानमिवानलम्।।1.11.15।।
shlokas.translation: He reached the spot and saw the son of the ascetic, the best of brahmins glowing like fire seated next to Romapada.

shlokas.original:
ततो राजा यथान्यायं पूजां चक्रे विशेषत:।
सखित्वात्तस्य वै राज्ञ: प्रहृष्टेनान्तरात्मना।।1.11.16।।
shlokas.translation: Then the king (Romapada), delighted at heart out of friendship with the king (Dasaratha), offered special hospitality with respectful salutations in accordance with propriety.

shlokas.original:
रोमपादेन चाख्यातमृषिपुत्राय धीमते।
सख्यं सम्बन्धकं चैव तदा तं प्रत्यपूजयत्।।1.11.17।।
shlokas.translation: Romapada explained his friendship and relationship with him (Dasaratha). Rsyasringa of great intellect in return felicitated Dasaratha.

shlokas.original:
एवं सुसत्कृतस्तेन सहोषित्वा नरर्षभ:।
सप्ताष्टदिवसान्राजा राजानमिदमब्रवीत्।।1.11.18।।
shlokas.translation: In this manner, the great among men, the king wellhonoured by Romapada, spent seven or eight days there.And then spoke this to Romapada:

shlokas.original:
शान्ता तव सुता राजन् सह भर्त्रा विशांपते।
मदीयनगरं यातु कार्यं हि महदुद्यतम्।।1.11.19।।
shlokas.translation: O lord of people, O king, It has been decided to commence an important sacrificial rite. You may send your daughter Santa along with her husband to my city.

shlokas.original:
तथेति राजा संश्रुत्य गमनं तस्य धीमत:।
उवाच वचनं विप्र��� गच्छ त्वं सह भार्यया।।1.11.20।।
shlokas.translation: Having agreed to the departure of the sage Romapada said, 'Be pleased to go (to Ayodhya) together with your wife'.

shlokas.original:
ऋषिपुत्र: प्रतिश्रुत्य तथेत्याह नृपं तदा।
स नृपेणाभ्यनुज्ञात: प्रययौ सह भार्यया।।1.11.21।।
shlokas.translation: The son of the sage assented to the king's command saying 'So be it'. And permitted by him, departed along with wife (for Ayodhya).

shlokas.original:
तावन्योन्याञ्जलिं कृत्वा स्नेहात्संश्लिष्य चोरसा।
ननन्दतुर्दशरथो रोमपादश्च वीर्यवान्।।1.11.22।।
shlokas.translation: Dasaratha and Romapada, pleased with this, wished each other with folded palms and hugged each other with love.

shlokas.original:
ततस्सुहृदमापृच्छ्य प्रस्थितो रघुनन्दन:।
पौरेभ्य: प्रेषयामास दूतान्वै शीघ्रगामिन:।।1.11.23।।
shlokas.translation: Then king Dasaratha, took leave of  his friend and set out. He despatched swiftfooted messengers to the citizens with the command.

shlokas.original:
क्रियतां नगरं सर्वं क्षिप्रमेव स्वलङ्कृतम्।
धूपितं सिक्तसम्मृष्टं पताकाभिरलङ्कृतम्।।1.11.24।।
shlokas.translation: "Let the entire city be welldecorated, perfumed, sprinkled with water and cleaned with flags hoisted."

shlokas.original:
तत: प्रहृष्टा: पौरास्ते श्रुत्वा राजानमागतम्।
तथा प्रचक्रुस्तत्सर्वं राज्ञा यत्प्रेषितं तदा ।।1.11.25।।
shlokas.translation: The people of the city having heard the king's arrival were overjoyed. They did  everything as by the king.

shlokas.original:
ततस्स्वलङ्कृतं राजा नगरं प्रविवेश ह।
शङ्खदुन्दुभिनिर्घोषै: पुरस्कृत्य द्विजर्षभम्।।1.11.26।।
shlokas.translation: Then the king entered that welldecorated city amidst blares of conches and the beating of drums, with the best of the brahmins (Rsyasringa) in the forefront.

shlokas.original:
तत: प्रमुदितास्सर्वे दृष्ट्वा तं नागरा द्विजम्।
प्रवेश्यमानं सत्कृत्य नरेन्द्रेणेन्द्रकर्मणा।।1.11.27।।
shlokas.translation: The citizens were delighted to behold that brahmin entering the city duly honoured by the king who was equivalent to Indra in valiance৷৷

shlokas.original:
अन्त:पुरं प्रवेश्यैनं पूजां कृत्वा च शास्त्रत:।
कृतकृत्यं तदात्मानं मेने तस्योपवाहनात्।।1.11.28।।
shlokas.translation: He (king Dasaratha) took him in to the inner apartment and extended welcome. He thought that he has as enjoined by the sastras accomplished his objective by bringing him (Rsyasringa).

shlokas.original:
अन्त:पुरस्त्रियस्सर्वाश्शान्तां दृष्ट्वा तथागताम्।
सह भर्त्रा विशालाक्षीं प्रीत्यानन्दमुपागमन्।।1.11.29।।
shlokas.translation: All the royal ladies, experienced immense delight when they saw the largeeyed Shanta arriving  along with her husband in that manner.

shlokas.original:
पूज्यमाना च ताभिस्सा राज्ञा चैव विशेषत:।
उवास तत्र सुखिता कञ्चित्कालं सहर्त्विजा।।1.11.30।।
shlokas.translation: Honoured and worshipped by royal ladies, especially by king Dasaratha, Santa dwelt there for some time comfortably with the sage. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डे एकादशस्सर्ग:।।  Thus ends the eleventh sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
